text: Unsolved black-hole problem #1: Spacetime singularities & their quantum-gravity resolution. General relativity predicts that gravitational collapse produces a spacetime singularity where curvature invariants diverge and the classical theory breaks down. The Penrose-Hawking theorems prove such singularities are generic (not artifacts of symmetry), given reasonable energy and causality conditions. What physically replaces the singularity is unknown and is believed to require a quantum theory of gravity, which does not yet exist in complete, predictive form. First posed 1916-1965 by Karl Schwarzschild, Roger Penrose, Stephen Hawking. Status: open. Key ideas: Curvature singularities are where geodesics are incomplete and invariants like the Kretschmann scalar diverge.; Penrose's trapped-surface argument makes singularity formation generic, independent of exact symmetry.; Cosmic censorship (problems #04, #05) concerns whether such singularities are hidden behind horizons.; Candidate resolutions replace r=0 with a quantum-gravity core: a bounce (loop quantum gravity), a fuzzball/string condensate, or a regular (non-singular) black-hole interior.; Any resolution must reduce to classical GR in the appropriate limit and respect the singularity theorems' assumptions or evade one of them (e.g. via quantum energy-condition violation)..
title: Spacetime singularities & their quantum-gravity resolution

text: On the Gravitational Field of a Mass Point According to Einstein's Theory (1916) by Karl Schwarzschild — foundational reference for black-hole problem #1 (Spacetime singularities & their quantum-gravity resolution). Sitzungsber. Preuss. Akad. Wiss.; the Schwarzschild solution.
title: On the Gravitational Field of a Mass Point According to Einstein's Theory

text: Gravitational Collapse and Space-Time Singularities (1965) by Roger Penrose — foundational reference for black-hole problem #1 (Spacetime singularities & their quantum-gravity resolution). Phys. Rev. Lett. 14, 57; the first modern singularity theorem (trapped surfaces).
title: Gravitational Collapse and Space-Time Singularities

text: The Singularities of Gravitational Collapse and Cosmology (1970) by Stephen Hawking, Roger Penrose — foundational reference for black-hole problem #1 (Spacetime singularities & their quantum-gravity resolution). Proc. R. Soc. Lond. A 314, 529.
title: The Singularities of Gravitational Collapse and Cosmology

text: The Large Scale Structure of Space-Time (1973) by Stephen Hawking, George F. R. Ellis — textbook reference for black-hole problem #1 (Spacetime singularities & their quantum-gravity resolution). Cambridge University Press; definitive treatment of the singularity theorems.
title: The Large Scale Structure of Space-Time

text: Quantum Nature of the Big Bang (2006) by Abhay Ashtekar, Tomasz Pawlowski, Parampreet Singh — recent reference for black-hole problem #1 (Spacetime singularities & their quantum-gravity resolution). loop-quantum-cosmology bounce; arXiv gr-qc/0602086.
title: Quantum Nature of the Big Bang

text: Roger Penrose (originator) — Singularity theorems via trapped surfaces and global causal methods; Nobel Prize 2020. Connected to black-hole problem #1 (Spacetime singularities & their quantum-gravity resolution).
title: Roger Penrose

text: Stephen Hawking (foundational) — Singularity theorems in cosmology and generic collapse. Connected to black-hole problem #1 (Spacetime singularities & their quantum-gravity resolution).
title: Stephen Hawking

text: Abhay Ashtekar (active) — Loop quantum gravity; quantum bounce replacing classical singularities. Connected to black-hole problem #1 (Spacetime singularities & their quantum-gravity resolution).
title: Abhay Ashtekar

text: Samir Mathur (active) — Fuzzball proposal for a non-singular black-hole interior. Connected to black-hole problem #1 (Spacetime singularities & their quantum-gravity resolution).
title: Samir Mathur

text: Demetrios Christodoulou (foundational) — Rigorous results on collapse and trapped-surface formation. Connected to black-hole problem #1 (Spacetime singularities & their quantum-gravity resolution).
title: Demetrios Christodoulou
